channel.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India
channel.description: World News Today: Read the latest world news headlines, Current International breaking news world wide. In depth analysis and top news headlines world wide.

image.title: World News, Today World News, Latest International News, World Breaking News, Trending News of World - Times of India

item.title: Finnish proverb of the day: "The forest answers in the same way one shouts at it" teaches us that we get what we do to others
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Kick Streamer Raja Jackson jailed following Kick livestream attack on wrestler Syko Stu
item.description: Kick streamer Raja Jackson has begun a ninety-day jail sentence for assaulting wrestler Syko Stu. The attack occurred during a live Kick broadcast in August of last year. Jackson must also pay eighty-one thousand dollars in restitution to Syko Stu. Syko Stu suffered severe injuries but has forgiven Jackson for the incident. The streamer will also serve two years of formal probation following his release.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Best proverb of the day: 'The ocean keeps every secret, but charges salt for storage'
item.description: The ocean stands as a vast archive, modifying its essence with every new element it encounters. This mirrors the way human memory and organizational structures evolve through absorbed experiences. Current scientific findings reveal rising carbon dioxide levels are reshaping ocean chemistry, leading to acidification. Moreover, relentless plastic pollution has invaded even the most secluded oceanic depths. Similarly, digital records and unresolved disputes in workplaces exemplify transformation in our lives.
item.dc:creator: TOI Trending Desk

item.title: F1 visa rejected on 214(b)? Former visa officer explains what to do now, before applying for it again
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Best proverb of the day: 'Even the sun negotiates with dust to make a shadow'
item.description: In every interaction, we witness the emergence of powerful forces, impressively reshaping themselves through their engagements with smaller components. Just as the sun interacts with dust to cast a distinct shadow, we see this dynamic at play in modern systems such as AI and workplaces. This principle underlines how constraints can nurture creativity and organizational structure, while personal identity emerges through the natural friction of experiences.
item.dc:creator: TOI Trending Desk

item.title: Less than 30% of Earth’s ocean floor has been mapped while scientists still have clearer high-resolution data of Mars than most of the seabed covering our own planet
item.description: Despite the vastness of Earth's oceans, only about thirty percent of the seafloor is charted to modern specifications, revealing a stark difference with Mars, whose surface boasts complete mapping. The Seabed 2030 initiative has dramatically improved ocean coverage from six to nearly twenty-nine percent. This meticulous mapping relies on ships and sonar, a time-intensive endeavor crucial for effective tsunami predictions and maintaining submarine cables.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: What did Sneako say about New York? Controversial remarks lead to deportation calls from Alex Jones
item.description: Sneako sparked controversy after a New York livestream in which he made comments about Islam and the future of the city. The clip spread widely on X and drew criticism from several public figures. Alex Jones called for the streamer’s deportation, while Gad Saad raised concerns about the scene shown in the video, further intensifying the online debate.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Who was TikTok star itgirlbri? Influencer dies after targeted Miramar shooting
item.description: TikTok influencer itgirlbri died following a targeted drive-by shooting in Florida. She was one of three people inside a Lamborghini SUV which came under gunfire. The vehicle was reportedly leaving an Airbnb party early on July 5. Police believe the Lamborghini was deliberately targeted by a white BMW. Authorities are actively searching for the suspects and the vehicle involved.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: What really happened between Jack Doherty and Lil Tjay? Livestream fight explained
item.description: What started as friendly banter between Jack Doherty and Lil Tjay during a Fourth of July Kick livestream quickly turned into a heated confrontation. Tensions rose over comments involving a watch, a chain and personal remarks about homes. Lil Tjay slapped Doherty first, and Doherty later appeared to slap him back before security intervened and the livestream suddenly ended.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Why was Kaceytron banned on Twitch? Streamer's channel goes offline amid suspension
item.description: Kaceytron's Twitch channel is currently offline due to an unexplained suspension. The platform has not revealed the specific guideline violation which led to this action. Online speculation points to a recent livestream where she was allegedly driving while intoxicated. Another theory suggests potential copyright infringement issues from past broadcasts. This marks Kaceytron's second known suspension from the streaming platform.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: In 1847, a Vienna doctor discovered that handwashing with chlorine could cut childbirth deaths by 90 percent, but the medical world refused to believe him
item.description: In a radical move, Ignaz Semmelweis mandated handwashing in hospitals, drastically reducing maternal mortality rates. Despite evidence supporting his approach, he faced fierce opposition from fellow medical professionals, as he couldn't provide a scientific rationale for his findings. Much later, the establishment of germ theory validated his revolutionary work, leading to fundamental changes in medical practices that ultimately saved innumerable lives.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Green Card news: Last updated in 2004, PERM regulations may change soon; employers will face new test
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists identify 74 genetic regions linked to anxiety in study of nearly 700,000 people, uncovering 39 never-before-seen DNA clues that could reshape future treatments
item.description: A groundbreaking genetic study uncovered seventy-four genome regions associated with anxiety symptoms, significantly expanding on previous research. The findings suggest that numerous small genetic variations contribute to the risk of anxiety. Additionally, a connection was identified between anxiety-related genes and physical health conditions, paving the way for innovative treatment strategies for anxiety disorders.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: Life lesson from proverb of the day: "One does not change a winning team" -- French wisdom teaches us why change is not always the answer
Cuba's national grid completely collapses, nearly 10 million in darkness
item.description: Cuba's national power grid collapsed, leaving ten million people without electricity. This nationwide blackout deepened the island's ongoing energy crisis. Ageing infrastructure and fuel shortages have caused frequent power outages for months. US sanctions are blamed by the government for limiting fuel imports. The situation has worsened amid a prolonged economic crisis on the island.
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: 'Closer than people realise': Trump says both Putin, Zelenskyy want war to end
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk

item.title: Scientists have found 99.7% chance of life in this planet and no, it's not Mars
item.description: Scientists detected gases on exoplanet K2-18b linked to life on Earth. The James Webb Space Telescope's future results show a tentative signal of dimethyl sulfide. This discovery is not yet confirmed and requires further observation for verification. K2-18b orbits its star in the habitable zone, making it a prime candidate. Future observations will determine if this represents a historic breakthrough in astrobiology.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: 300,000-year-old Homo naledi fossils from a South African cave suggest the ancient human relative may have been a female-only species, new study reveals
item.description: A recent examination of ancient dental remains from South Africa's cave systems has led to a striking conclusion. Proteomic analysis revealed solely female protein markers in the samples examined. This intriguing finding implies that all identified Homo naledi individuals could potentially be female. Researchers are currently delving into possible explanations for this unexpected trend, which raises significant questions regarding established beliefs about the species' gender distribution.
item.dc:creator: TOI Science Desk

item.title: “I don’t fear these…”: Candace Owens reignites Erika Kirk feud during Tyler Robinson hearing in Charlie Kirk case
item.description: Candace Owens reacted strongly as Tyler Robinson’s preliminary hearing began on July 6. She called the proceedings a “show trial,” questioned how evidence is being handled and criticized the court process. In a second post, she shared a faith-based message dedicated to Charlie Kirk. The hearing will determine whether prosecutors have enough evidence to move the case to trial.
item.dc:creator: Sumit Roy

item.title: Man seen openly defecating at Cedar Park neighborhood in Texas; major outrage on social media
item.dc:creator: TOI World Desk
